sentences.text: Hello?
Bye!
Hi, how are you?
Good, how are you?
Doing well.
I can even just share my screen.
Oh, perfect.
Here, I'll take off my finger out.
I have, Ruby in here with me, but just based on the invites, I can't necessarily share it up on screen, so… You know.
I'll.
Oh, good.
How was the weekend?
Doing anything fun?
Labor Day?
I had a wedding out of town, so it was fast and busy.
That's relaxation for me.
I am the same way.
My husband thinks it's so weird.
It's always, like, the longest work days I have where I vacuum the whole house.
That's relaxing for me more than, like, sitting down.
A wedding, Labor Day weekend.
I don't know how I feel about that.
I'm like, I love it because you already have the day off on Monday, but it kind of takes away any plans you could have.
Yeah, and it was my first, wedding with a kid, too, and I… quickly learned how challenging that is.
I saw a whole new side of my son I've never seen.
Oh, gosh.
Not the best way.
So, and they didn't squash there either, too, so I think that added to the chaos.
I'm a big believer that you need to try to put your kids in situations in which they can thrive, and a wedding is not one of those situations.
Like, I know, like, he's… I know last time we were just talking, I was like, he's so easy, he's got the flow, and it was just, like, way too much for him.
And of course, we stayed in a house with my family with his two cousins, who are two and four, so it was, like, constant stimulation.
Wedding time, he's like, I'm done.
Like, I'm an only child, what is this?
What are you doing?
Where was the wedding?
San Diego.
Oh, fun.

Yeah, but that was the other half of it, of… it was beautiful, overlooking the ocean, but it's a wall that he could very easily claw over, so the whole time, just making sure he's not falling into the ocean, so it was… That's fun.
I've never been to San Diego.
It's one of my biggest regrets in life.
I should probably get there.
It's not too late!
San Diego's the best.
It is.
I've also never been to Europe, so I think I'd prioritize Europe over that.
Have you, Katrina?
Not Europe, no, yeah, I've only done, like, Caribbean kind of area.
Damn.
We've all traveled.
Kylie, if you ever need Europe.
Recommendations.
Kylie is one of the most well-traveled people you'll ever meet.
Wow, that's quite… quite the compliment.
At least that I know of.
I mean, in my world, you're very well traveled.
I try to.
I try to do at least one big trip a year.
The last couple, I've done, like, two international trips a year, so if I can hold that cadence moving forward, I'll be happy.
Yeah.
Please do.
That's what it's all about.
Yep.
Well, well, thank you guys for jumping on.
So, we've been in chats with this team, for, I guess.
greater part of the year about our social automation product, but we've been working with RPM for just over a year now on our Google Business Automation product.
So, been working with Kyle and his team over there.
We just hit 500 properties live with the Google Business Tool.
So, it's been going really well, so excited to continue chats on the social product with this team.
So, I guess before I jump in, Katrina, have Ruby and Liv, have you two seen the product at all?
Have you heard anything about it?
Kind of what made you just start from the top and what we're doing?
Yeah, I joined on a meeting.
I feel like it was, like, my first month that I joined RPL.
Got a very small glimpse of it, but, I don't know all the features and everything that it does, but… Yeah.
Excited to learn more.
Okay.
Yeah, same.
I'm very excited to… to learn all that you're hopefully gonna teach us today.
This sounds like a lot of fun.
Yeah.
Well, I'm excited to show you guys, too, and yes, Ruby, I remember that.
I looked, it was back in January, so I was like, so, so long ago, I feel like there's… there's a lot that has changed since then, too, so… Cool, well, I'll give you.
I mean, I…
Oh, sorry, go ahead.
Oh, sorry, Kylie.
Katrina, when… from our conversation, just triple checking, Ruby and this team, this is a team that's in Sochi every day, kind of doing… Okay.
Yeah, so essentially, like, Ruby replaced Emily, who's still with us, and Liv replaced Jordan.
So, yeah, obviously, I don't think Emily ever did it, but yeah, Jordan heavily, Did, obviously, the pilot before, so just to drop comparison.
Jordan, the Phoenix Suns mascot.
Yeah, yeah.
Favorite thing about him, yeah.
I am.
So, is it the two of you that are in Sochi the most, creating content, scheduling content?
Yeah.
All of the above.
what does that process look like right now?
Like, how do you determine what days you're posting, what content you're creating?
Is it just the two of you, or what does that whole process look like?
Yeah, it's just the two of us for properties that are enrolled.
I think, how many properties do we have under us?
I know that I have lots of… yeah, between, like, 30 to 50.

Yeah, and so we like to plan our content a month ahead, and Lib and I, we just met yesterday to plan for October, so we'll like to do, like, you know, seasonal themes and everything, but also tie it back to the community, so focusing on the amenities, in-home features, apartment living tips.
more generalized for, property-specific items.
And then, once we have our captions ready, our topics ready, we go through our SharePoint folder to, you know, gather photos from the property itself.

But we also utilize Canva to upload, additional photos and create reels and other graphics as well, and then upload that content to SoSheet and schedule it out based on, maybe, like, their peak times as well.

The one piece I'll add is, I know, Jordan, you know, had that very prescriptive method, I think we talked about, like, general versus more customized, but similar to what you all reviewed with Sam and I, like, last week.
Ruby has created a format where it is, it's like.

to community amenity-focused, to, like, in-unit focused, you know, like, so she has, like, the 8, kind of similar to how you have your 8 defined, like, we have buckets, and then, yeah, like, pull and seasonal relevance, other things of that nature.
So we always do one neighborhood, we'll do a pet-friendly, we'll do a… etc.
Yep.
And are you typically doing it twice a week?
That's what you… I know you scheduled it a month ahead of time, but is it typically, like, two posts a week that are going out for each property?
Typically, yes.
Okay.
Okay.
So, this process would automate a lot of what you're doing for those types of posts.
So, really, the tool and the reason that we've had customers roll this out is to save teams time from manually creating content.
schedule out those post types that you know you're going to be making every month.
So, the community amenities, the unit-level amenities, the holiday posts.
So that's why customers have rolled it out, is to save teams time from the manual, aspect.
But then also to ensure you have consistent posts going out.
The posts are property-specific.
And your profiles are fully optimized.
So… I can jump in and show you what it looks like.

One more question before you do that, and Katrina, maybe you covered it, but this crew here, outside of posting on social, any… what other kind of aspects of the role are they… are you guys working on?

That's the largest focus, but we have, like, ticketing that we do, new business, like, just analyzing social posts, and then otherwise, they're creating resources for our teams, and that is either drafting a few posts that are generalized enough for everyone to use, so this is kind of where, like I mentioned to you.

teams who opt into Sochi have to actually go schedule those posts, where this would automatically do it for them, or there's a secondary resource where we give them inspiration, but they still have to, in that one, create their own caption and find the image.
So, there's still action on their end.
None of this is, like, a set it and forget it for this, like, requirement we have.
Yeah, okay, that makes sense.
Cool.
Cool.
Well, like I said, all of it's built to automate, a lot of those tasks, and how we're getting this information is directly from your property management softwares.
So that's another piece of this, is you don't have to have, folders or files of the content to send us.
We're pulling it all directly from your property management softwares.

So, it's like the source of truth for all the information that we're pulling, both floor plans that are available right now, photos, amenity-based information, all of that's coming directly from the feed.
And then we're layering on top of that our in-house built AI tool to create the content.
So, using the feed for the information, and then our tool can actually read the photo and match the content to that photo.
So, every post that goes out has a photo, and then that content is built around that photo and what's being displayed in that photo.
So, like I said, select the content, so it goes into the feed, and we are able to pull amenity fields.

So, we're not just reading the photo and making up an amenity name, we're actually taking the amenity fields that your feed says you have, and then finding the photo and matching the photo, and then creating the content on top of that.
So the post types, that are going out, and this was different from when you saw it, Ruby, is… We have… we're posting twice a week now as, like, our standard… standard way we have it set up.
That's actually what most of our customers told us that they were targeting for their teams.
So, we used to do it 3 times, but we've switched it a little bit to make sure that the quality of the content is there, and not just posting to post.
So, what we're targeting now is twice a week.

Thursday and Friday… er, Tuesday and Thursday is, like, the standard way we have it set up, but I will say this tool is built in-house, so it gives us the ability to customize it and tweak it if those… if you wanted those days to be shifted.
And what you're seeing here is the… are the post types.
So, one of each of these a month.
So…
what you were kind of saying with the community-level amenities, the in-unit-level amenities, highlighting a floor plan.
So those are all, like, the property-specific… and lifestyle, those are, like, the property-specific posts.
And then we're also layering in tour scheduling posts, highlighting pet policies.
Highlighting a holiday, if there's a holiday that falls within that month.
And then we're also able to highlight local attractions.
Gotcha, yeah, very similar to our template that we created as well.
Yeah.

Yep, so the differences here is you would log into, what Jordan was doing when we did a pilot on this was he would log in to the dashboard to review all of the automated posts that were coming, but at that time, he could only see, I think we were, like, 7 or 8 days out of posts.
So that's also one difference here, is we're… you can now see the next 30 days of posts.
So, similar to your process, where you're going in, scheduling the next 30 days, but you'd be logging into here, and you already have a preloaded, view of the next 30 days of posts that are coming.
So, all 8 posts for this conversation.
All 8 posts would be, pre-loaded in here for you to see for that month.
How much, and you might get into the demo, has this changed in the sense, like, two things come to mind.

Are we able to, like, swap the photo out, so if we're like, yeah, we're okay, you know, posting about the clubhouse, but use a different photo, or, like, the drag and drop, like, I know sometimes, like, Amazon Locker kept coming up, which we were like, actually, we don't mind an extra post, because it's holiday season, so it's relevant, but let's just move it so it's not so close to the last one.
Is a little bit of that unaddressed, too, or not quite?
There have been changes there where, when you log into the dashboard, here's a view of what it looks like.
Here, let me just go to social for this.
So, you'll see all of the preloaded posts.
In here, you can edit posts.
So you can change the content that's here if you wanted to, add, remove, you can swap the photo out as well.
So that would be one part of the answer, is that you can swap the photos.
Great.
You can also deny a post.
So if there's a post that you just didn't want to go out, you can hit this deny button, and a new post will be generated for that day.
So, right in its place.
You could change the date, or no?
It might be grayed out.
Okay, so it looks like you can change it.
You can change it, yeah.
So I could change this to go out on Friday instead of Thursday.
Okay, cool.
Go ahead.
Sorry, go ahead.
I was gonna say, what about content that's already been published?
I know with Sochi, there's a limitation where we can't delete or edit Instagram posts that have already been published.
We have to do that on the back end through Meta.
Is that something we can do here?
So you can view all of the published posts?
Patrick, do you know if you can edit them?
No, it's like a meta limitation.
So, any software, we'd love to go back, and Google, we can, but once it's on Instagram and Facebook, it's on Instagram and Facebook, you'd have to go into your Meta account and delete it.
Okay.
Yeah, it's definitely a meta thing, not us.
Which is why Sochi kind of has the same problem.
Kylie, with the last thing you were doing, the deny and regenerate, how long does it take to create the new post?
Because I remember last time it was, like, a 2-hour kind of time frame.
I think it's still about that time.
I know it's not immediate.
I can ask to see if it's sped up any more than the 2 hours, but I think there is still a little bit of a lag.
What?
Okay, cool.
But I'll ask our engineering team.
it's still the same, yeah.
I think most of our customers, when they deny They do it further out, so I wouldn't recommend day of, but… the workaround is just the edit directly right there.
Slide in another picture.
Quickly edit the text.
Because the refresh takes, you know, about an hour or so, I think.
Cool.

Yeah, I… sometimes it was usually… I think you guys quoted 2 hours, but it often did tend to be within that hour time frame, but yeah, it just came… that was… again, it wasn't a rolling 30 days at that point, so we would be ahead, ideally.
Yeah.

I think the other thing we think about that's maybe new since you last worked with us is just brand guidelines at the property level, so it sounds like this team does a lot of work around supporting digital assets that are property-specific.
So, if you have guidelines.
or ways that you want posts to sound at the property level, we can accommodate that.
If you want posts to be shorter, or longer, or have a specific call to action, or be sure to have specific hashtags in them, we can get Customizable with this at the property level.
Which has been big for our customers.
I've moved from a more manual approach to adding automation.
So, there is… there is capabilities to handle… we know each property maybe has a different brand kit or brand guidelines, so we can… we can incorporate that… incorporate that.

I think to that point, we talked about, like, how we have a branded post, and so all we do is we take the same property photo you'd be referencing, but throwing it in Canva and adding, like, a logo overlay or color block to it or something.
Is there a way we can create, like, we want one post a month to pull a photo that is branded?

Like, so maybe it's how we title the photos, but yeah, like, theoretically right now, depending upon the package, you'll have anywhere from, like, one to four branded posts, so… I know that'd be an upfront thing we would have to do, where we would have to provide you those branded photos, but if we tag it correctly and set the parameters, could it pull.
Yeah.
options in the flow.
I think the tagging is the most important part, but yeah, I think we could accommodate that.
Do you have… I know that you have a large portfolio, but do you have an example of a branded post I could take a look at?
I've got a property, and I could just search in the background here.
Yeah, I can just share, like, on Instagram to everyone.
Just…
Yeah.
What's their handle?
I'm gonna have to log back into my Instagram.
I think that would go to more, like, the lifestyle post, Kylie, like, when you get back to the… when you look at slide 6, I think that's, like, a fluid post type.
That could be more branded.

I know we do… I know we do that with another customer, where they… they're working with us on Google and social, and for the Google, we're pulling in photos from the feed, but they wanted their social-specific photos to be branded, so they just sent us a Dropbox link.
And then those are the photos that we're pulling in for the social posts.
The only… the only part of that question I don't know for sure is, like, if you just wanted one of those a month.
If we could, like, set that specific cadence where we're pulling from the feed for everything else, but only from Dropbox once.
Okay, that could work.
I bet we could.
I think we… I know we do this for RKW.
They had the same thing.
the Everson Apartments?
Absolutely.

At the very top.

Guidebar, or even just, like, yeah, the logo over it.

Wow.
Property photography, and just adding in some brand elements to it.
Yeah, I don't see why we couldn't, and haven't…

post.

I think, yeah, there's an MP4 you are working on.

I should know this, but it's always reels, right?

Correct.

And did this… this… again, the one with the girl with the headphones, would that go to multiple properties, or does that just go to the Douglas?

Their day-to-day, the overarching company expectation, then also, like.
Yes.
unique instances, like we talked about Crisis or this campaign where we have to bulk post as well.
And that can be achieved because you have basically all properties subscribe to Sochi, but not necessarily all of them get the, your team's services, right?
Exactly, that's when… that's where we create groups or different things.

Like, we created a custom summer club group, or sometimes for crisis, they have, like, smart grouping, so, like, they can pull up by city, so when you know, okay, a hurricane's impacting this area, that's helped us create a group faster.
That is something I'm curious, too.
So, if we have something scheduled through a group, bulk scheduled, and it has not gone live yet, if we make an edit, does it apply all the edits?
Because that was really nice with Sochi, where we had to make a few edits after we'd already scheduled everything, but once we applied the edit.
As long as the post didn't go out, it applied to everyone that had it… had been scheduled through that group.
Yeah, the bulk post, as long as it hasn't gone out yet, when it's edited, would apply to the entirety of the bulk post.
Yeah.
Yeah, and so, not to belabor the girl with the headphones, but the 400, those properties were enrolled in your services, Katrina.
This one's a campaign that our services supported.
your team supported it.
Hmm.
Got it.
Okay.
Just trying to… I mean, you know, obviously you have multiple ways.
Sounds like you have, like.
the crisis side, where you'd have to have access.
If you didn't enroll 400 properties with our platform, you had 800 or whatever that didn't have our platform, you had the crisis.
scenario, you'd have to figure out a way, maybe you still had some… I don't know, you'd have to figure out a way to get those out.
Yeah, that's where we just say, here's our recommended caption and photo, do it yourself natively, but that's hence the Sochi piece.
But yeah, so in this instance, we did.
We had, like, I don't know, 8 properties that were enrolled in, and I provided them a drop box and the captions for them to do it themselves.
But yeah, I can tell you, like, crisis every year.
And then, this campaign happens every year for 2 months long.
It's usually 4th of July to Labor Day, where we're doing that, so…
And they got, I think you said, 12 posts for the campaign, was that right?
We did 2, I think it was 18 who did it, because it's 9 weeks… it was 9 weeks long, or maybe it was…
And… Alright, I'm getting way in the weeds with you.
I just have a couple more questions.
Hmm.
So, on Kyle's side.
when they sign up for a Google package, they sign, like, an agreement for the, like, the agreement to enroll in the services.

Yep.
And then do they typically turn it off after that, or do they stay on because they want more posts from you?
Typically, it's, like, a one and done, because this is just one component of the larger agreement, because that's where the commercial.
Oh my god.
placement on Pinterest, etc, like, so it's a much broader effort.
But yeah, ideally, they could see how nice it is to actually have their feed filled with great content, and then would want to enroll with us.
Got it, okay.
That makes sense.
Yeah, that's right.

I'm kind of looking at it, like, I think I told you whether this creates efficiencies for them, so we could theoretically handle more properties with our same headcount if we had, like, if we did an enrollment push, or… where I'm more interested in is an opportunity, which comes into a much broader conversation, but, like, a replacement to Sochi in the sense, I just like it.
Where, right now, we have a lot of properties who are enrolled and paying for it, and are doing zero-to-one posts.
Yeah, we see it all the time.
Yeah, exactly.
So it's like, at least they're getting something, even if it's not perfect, and I know at least you put a lot of effort into quality over quantity, but even before.
it was doing a pretty good job with ChatGBD compared to some of what we see when people are even just trying, period.
But yeah, so I'm excited to see with the quality measures you've made that it's… it would be a really good option for them.
Because now, on Kyle's side, it was like… it was a big efficiency thing, and they can do.
I'm gonna…
properties on Google, and they get a ton of content, and Google's a different beast than social, but it just.

But that's part of it, like, it's changing, like, so we know we want Google to be constantly showing us up-to-date, but now it's social, like, the landscape's changing for social, so that's why I want more of Again, it's kind of taking the onus off the on-site team, who already has a million things and are stretched thin.
For them to not have to worry, and making sure that they're helping our marketing efforts more broadly speaking.
Yeah, I think you're right.
I think the lines between, like, organic Google approach with… And organic social posting is kind of getting blurred as they all kind of factor.
Yeah.
It's a local search, so that would be a benefit of just consistent property-specific content at scale.
And then he does.
like, portfolio-wide or region-based?
So, like, let's say we partnered with Ben, and he would like the… could you apply it maybe regionally?

Keyword is all, like, if he wants… not keyword stuffing, obviously, but, like, if he wants to ideally work in certain keywords to help SEF… SEO efforts on top of everything else we're trying to accomplish, is that something that can be, like, a directive given?
If, if Ben wanted keywords in your social posts.
Just to help you out towards SEO efforts.
Yeah.
Definitely.
Okay.
Yeah, I think… I think you can… we… there's a couple factors we can take in.
We can take in phrases and keywords that you want in the captions.
We can totally accommodate that.
You know, whether it's, like, location or an amenity that you're trying to highlight.
And then…
we can incorporate some, like, brand guidelines, too, and how you want to speak about it.
So there's, like, two kind of inputs that we can put into the post.
So, yes, we can incorporate keywords here, too.
Cool.
So, sorry, I know we wanted to show you more of the platform, just… Curious about, you know, digging into your strategy a little bit, so…
For sure.
Yeah, well, a couple other of the features that I did want to show, just on the dashboard, one of them being an export button.
So, just so you're aware, you can also export these posts.
So, if you're, like, it's easier for you to review the next 30 days of posts by exporting it, or you wanted to schedule, send it to, yourselves or anyone else, you can do that.
That's a feature that actually RPM helped us build on the Google side, so we have it on social as well.
And then the New Post button.
So, this is the place that you can go in and manually create posts.
So you can drag and drop videos or photos into here, and select the photos that you want this to go out to.
Drag and drop your content here, and then you can schedule send as well.
So…
We've seen customers, yeah.
So you can log in, see all the automated posts that are going out, what days they're going out, and then determine where you want your additional manual posts to go out, right in this dash.
I will share, Livin Ruby.

We already talked about, with their automation, they're exploring, like, app mention that we do for neighborhoods, and then the other piece, they're also looking at music, knowing that it's a limitation we have with, Sochi as well.
Those are two things I brought up when I met with them last week.
Do those videos have music, by the way?
Back to the girl with the headphones?
I promise this is my last film.
Yeah.
But yeah, that's exactly it.
Like, when we schedule through Sochi, as long as the music is already on the file, it's fine, but you can't add it once you put it in Sochi.
I think there are some licensing agreements and licensing issues there to… Our team was looking at, so… still digging there.
Surprised me, yeah.
which we're working on, I mean, it's the current limitation of SOCHI, and…
Ruby and I were just reviewing a resource to help guide people of how to work around it, so… It's, you know, that seems to be a little bit of Instagram's API game.
Yeah, for sure.
So, theoretically, when Libby and Liv and Ruby want to say the go-ahead, they would give you the properties, we would get it connected.

how quickly it would generate… it would probably generate, obviously, all 8 at once for them to review, so it might be a quick review, but then you said it's, like, rolling, like, each week to a new post-populate type deal, or just trying to set, like.
I mean, they… them within their bandwidth, like, when they want to have this go live, knowing that probably at the very beginning, it'll be a quick review.
I mean, obviously, it's only 8 posts, that's not the worst thing, but…
What would be the best way for you guys to review those?
If we had it, like, a rolling 8, or if it had the full month come out at once, and then there was a delay to the next group?
I don't know, I think, to me, it'd probably be nice to see the whole month rolled out, just because we are working month to month.
Yep.
I think that's the way that we have it set up as well.
So, typically it's rolling in the fact that you can go in right now and you can see the next 8 posts.
But then they are continuously being generated on the back end, but you don't have to continuously review them.
You could go in again after to the next month.
So you review the next month coming out, and then they get generated, but then you can review them one month out.
Does that make sense, the way I'm explaining it?
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, I think what we'll have to do is we'll get out with our team, because right now we have an RPM login, but you're going to see 500 Google properties, which you don't want to see.
You'll want a separate… I don't think you'll want to see that.
You'll want a separate social… Login, so you just see for the two that you pilot, just the two that… You can, you can review the posts.
So we'll make sure we denote that internally.
I did have a quick question, though.
